news.text: Trump urges Iran to sign a deal after report suggests US may extend blockade - Reuters
Gold slips as oil prices fuel inflation fears ahead of Fed chair remarks - Reuters
Swiss bank UBS exceeds profit forecasts as Iran war stokes trading volatility - Reuters
Trump threatens Iran with AI picture of himself with a gun: 'No more Mr. Nice guy!'
Iran war to supercharge Asia's move from fossil fuels to electrons - Reuters
Iran war, jet fuel concerns cloud airlines' summer holiday plans - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Pre-market action shows a classic risk-off rotation with tech bleeding (-1.69%) while defensive sectors find buyers. The VIX at 28.33 signals elevated fear, but it's actually down 1.94%, suggesting some volatility compression. Iran tensions are driving energy (+1.66%) and creating a defensive bid in staples and REITs.

◆ What to Watch

  • Fed Chair Remarks - Powell speaks today with gold already slipping on inflation concerns and oil price pressures
  • Housing Data Triple-Header - MBA mortgage data at 11:00 AM followed by Building Permits at 12:30 PM could signal housing market stress
  • Durable Goods Orders - Ex-defense numbers at 12:30 PM will show manufacturing demand strength
  • Iran-Trump Escalation - Trump's latest threats and potential blockade extensions keeping oil bid and risk assets pressured

◆ Sector Rotation

Energy is the clear winner as Iran war concerns boost crude prices and refining margins. Tech is getting hammered on multiple fronts - higher rates expectations and geopolitical risk-off. The defensive rotation into utilities (+0.13%), staples (+0.90%), and REITs (+0.97%) tells you everything about sentiment.
⚡ Key Driver
Iran crisis reshaping energy markets. UAE leaving OPEC while Iran tensions escalate creates supply shock fears. Watch crude futures for market direction.
⚠ Bear Case
Housing data could crater if mortgage rates spike further. Building permits and MBA data at risk of major misses with rates climbing on Iran war inflation fears.
✦ Bull Case
VIX declining despite headlines suggests professional money isn't panicking. UBS beating forecasts shows financial resilience. This could be a fade-the-fear setup.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY $708 - Pre-market low and key support from last week's consolidation
  • QQQ $650 - Major technical level; break triggers accelerated tech selling
  • XLE $95 - Energy breakout level; clear this and momentum players pile in
  • VIX 30 - Psychological resistance; punch through signals real panic mode

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
